I want to kind of tell you some of my backstories a lot of people ask me like how do you how do you learn all this stuff? What's what's you know? Why do you do this? And so it all began for me when I was about 12 years old and that's when my junk male addiction began I was actually watching late night TV one night My my dad was watching the news and and I think you forgot that I that I was still awake And when the news was over all of a sudden these little infomercials came up and there was this guy on this infomercial Talking about how he made money posting classified ads in in newspapers And I got so excited that I wanted to learn how to do it And so I wanted to I wanted my dad to buy the kit my dad's like I'm not gonna buy that kit But I was sold a the guy sold me on on the fact you can make money Using classified ads and so I went and I saved up $40 first kid I had a moulons and do a bunch of work and I saved up money and I I bought this kit and the kit He started talking about how he was making money with classified ads. He said basically he said I I took a classified ad I placed it in a newspaper and when I did I made $30 profit He said and then he said I kind of I was proud of that but I told my friends and my family and they kind of made fun of me and We said I took that same ad and I placed it a thousand newspapers and then I was able to make $30,000 and I remember when he explained that like the light bulbs went off my head I realized that man if you understand marketing and how to create products and and do things like that you can literally scale a Business really really big like I was thinking about people You know my teachers I'm thinking about other people like that who go to work each day and they're working for you know 10 or 12 or 15 or $50 or $100 an hour or even more But no matter what they want to make more money They got to work more hours and this was my big first epiphany that man if I create a product or a service and I sell it I can sell it all day long and then doesn't increase that the hours I have to work and That was kind of the beginning of that my home marketing my home marketing journey and again I was 12 years old at the time and so I started going out there and I started studying this and learning things I remember I bought a magazine called small business opportunities magazine and some of you guys have probably seen it before it's this It's literally a magazine that has a hundred 140 pages worth of ads and like two articles inside of it and And I remember I was flipping through this reading all these ads and it just got me excited I started calling the 1 800 numbers in these ads and requesting their free kits and I started learning about about marketing And this is what that old offline method kind of look like Okay, somebody would see an ad like me in a magazine or a newspaper or they get a postcard or something Right and they would call a phone number and then somebody answer the phone and they say hey You know thanks for calling for your free kit. What's your address and they get the address and then that person will then mail them physically mail them a letter And that letter they would open it up and what was it? It was a sales letter and that sells there within sell some kind of product Okay, and this is where I learned back when I was 12 years old that this is how people were making money Okay, the guy in the infomercial was placing classified ads and doing this process. I saw people in magazines doing I started getting direct mail I started just learning all these things and this is the way that they're making money There's some kind of direct response ads saying call this phone number or mail in you know a self-addressed stamped envelope to this thing And they would mail back a free report and the free report was just a sales letter They would sell them some kind of product and remember I I saw that over and over and over again, and I got it I said this is awesome, but I didn't know how to do that. I didn't have any money to go by ads it was really expensive and and And so while I learned that I didn't have the ability to ever do it And I think that that's one thing that's that's unfortunate a lot of people are never able to to to actually make money Back then because it just costs too much and that's the beauty of the internet a few years later When I wasn't 12 when I was closer to 20 21 or 22 so about 10 years later. I started learning about this whole internet thing It was interesting is as I started learning about it. It was very similar to the offline thing Except instead of having to buy an ad in a newspaper or a magazine or on TV or whatever We're buying ads on websites and somebody would click on that ad and we go to a page It would say hey, do you want a free report? Give me your email address Okay, notice is very similar to a real address, but give me your email address And I'll email you this report people give you the email address they click submit in the next page What would show up a report? What was that report? It was a sales letter selling them something Okay, this looks familiar guys This process is happening offline is the exact same process that's happening online The difference is that for me to go place and add it's a lot cheaper sometimes for me to send somebody a sales letter an email is free versus a Direct mail cost a lot more money and suddenly when I learned this process I saw I said this is the same thing I was learning about when I was 12 But now it's on the internet now people like me can play okay, and this again. I was 21 22 years old I was in college and I was learning this and it got me excited because I Realized this something that I could actually do I could do this And so I started getting into this whole business started Started doing this process I'd write ads and I'd send them to a page and I get them to join my list and I'd send themselves letters and sales videos to Sell them stuff and I started started Selling all sorts of things started growing my own company and started having a ton of fun with that So that's kind of where for me this whole this whole internet market thing began Okay, so I wanted to tell that story so really want you guys to see the correlations that all this stuff I'm talking about and we're gonna be showing you guys today a Lot of it's very similar to what what's been happening for hundreds of years like we're not inventing this But it's a new media and it's a new way to do it when you understand that and understand how to harness it You can scale a company very very quickly in fact for example, we launched a new supplement a little while ago and In January of this year the supplement was doing about twenty thousand dollars a month And as soon as we got our numbers right and kind of this process figured out and it started working We were able to scale it by March We were making three hundred thousand a month within a month later month or two later at five hundred thousand dollars a month And next month we have the potential to do over a million dollars and that's happening in you know Within just a couple months we can scale things very very quickly and that's that's the power of this whole online stuff We're talking about and this is why I think people like the internet now because you can scale things up so much faster than you ever could So much faster and cheaper than you ever could with with traditional offline marketing. Okay, so many people and and I I'm guilty of this as well We get in this entire business and we don't think about About who we really want to sell to initially we kind of come in and maybe we have an idea for a product or service We start creating things and we start just moving forward Or maybe we already have an offline business and we just want to take it online We start running and what I found this is a lesson that man took me probably eight years To learn and I wish I would have learned eight years earlier Which is why we're starting with it because if you if you do this correctly You can literally design your dream business from day number one and have it become a business you love Okay, if you don't do this, you're probably gonna have happened to you what kind of happened to me You're gonna build a business that Won't that that seems really nice at first but because you didn't really plan it out it very quickly becomes more of a nightmare and And I want to save you guys from that from that turmoil and term it by by figuring out design your business the right way initially and so This is the the first step in that is a process. I can I can recall exactly what it was that I was trying to learn Okay, so this is a secret formula to building your dream business. Okay, now if you don't know who that person is right now, I promise you you're gonna track the wrong person, okay? For years I was just selling stuff about how does how to make money on the internet I didn't really identify who my target market was I said hey anyone wants to make money on the internet That's who I'm selling to and guess what happened from that I got everyone who wanted to make money on the internet and a lot of those people the majority of those people Didn't have any money. They didn't have any time. They didn't have any skills and any resources And they just weren't the kind of people who were gonna have success Okay, and it wasn't until about two years ago. We we sat down and said look this is not our dream client Okay, these are not kind of people who want to work for they who do we actually want to work with? And we sat down we started thinking like who is that person who's our dream client? And we started mapping out exactly that person is and what they what they're interested in what they like and what their passions are And and we started mapping out all these things and we really identified who are who our target customer was and I want to show you something kind of cool because The next two slides I think will be interesting to you guys because I'm curious that if these slides Represent who you are okay because what we did is went through we mapped out all the the idea all the things about our dream customer and then I actually went to Google and and I and I we kind of picked a We call us the customer avatar right and we picked a name for each one and so Like for example first I said it's gonna be Julie. This is what she's gonna look like This is what she's gonna be interested in we kind of wrote that out Then I took Julie and I went to Google and I went to Google images I typed in Julie successful business woman and all the different Criteria of what I wanted and I click submit and all these pictures started showing up and I scrolled through it till I found My dream client. Thank you take that Yeah, so I created bait that that my dream client would be attracted to it's just like I'm not a big fisher person Let's say you want to go out and you knew exactly the type of fish you wanted to catch Every fish has different bait right okay, so if you throw the wrong bait in you're gonna get the wrong fish You throw the right bait in you're gonna get exactly who you want Okay, so after I've identified who my person is I gotta find out where those people are hanging out and then I create bait That's gonna attract my dream client. I throw that bait in there and now I can get my dream client That's how I get them now after I've got them. I gotta figure out where to want to take them Okay, too many of businesses online on offline and I'm sure too many of your guys of businesses You're focusing so much on like what's the product I can sell what's a product? What's a product but what you need to be focusing on is where can I take my dream my dream client? Okay, where can I take that person to serve him at the highest level because that's that's a business Okay, there's a big difference between a product and a business a product is something you sell and you make some money with a business is When you when you're working with somebody you're taking somebody from from where they are to somewhere else Okay, and and this is kind of a big step We're gonna talk about that this is well throughout the the trading. Okay, but that's the secret formula you guys Initially you need to think about that and folks that who do I who's my dream client where those people at? What's the bait? Alright, okay So I was stepping one step number two now is what I call the value ladder and this is I think when I first learned this concept And start understanding it. This was one of the biggest ah ha's for me It helped me to really take our company to it to a whole different level almost overnight And it's a concept that that too many businesses don't understand and usually my first start working people I want them to understand this piece of it because if you understand this piece Almost the rest almost all the rest of it will just kind of take care of itself Okay, so this is called the value ladder now Again the first time I started to kind of learning about this I was at a seminar I heard this guy named Mike Mike Littman talk and Mike was talking about his business model He said hey, I give away a free CD to get customers in and then from there I sell them my coaching I thought okay that makes sense. He said guess how much I how much money I pay to give away a free CD I was like, I don't know two bucks three bucks. He said on average. I'm paying between 30 and $60 to give away For every free CD that I give away And in my head I was like, are you stupid? Why would you do that? You're gonna pay someone $60 to give away a free CD like that makes no logical sense for me And I remember like the wheels in my head turning and then Mike got kind of quiet and he leaned into the mic He said do you want to why I do that? I was thinking again. This is me as a 21 year old kid I said, you know what why would he do that? He said because amateurs focus on the front end Amateurs focus on the front end He said to to build a business the right way you have to understand your building a business not just a product Okay, so because my business is structured the right way I can I can spend 30 or 40 or $50 to give away a free CD and it makes sense because I'm gonna make that money up on The back end. Okay, so amateurs focus on the front end in the inverse of that is people like us who understand business Understand that we're here to make money that we're professionals and we're trying to grow a company We understand that we focus on the back end. All right, so we hear that a lot, but what does that mean? This is a good example from a company. I'm sure you're all familiar with called McDonald's Okay, and I McDonald's if you look at right now, they spent about a dollar 91 In advertising to get somebody to come through there the drive through window. Okay when they show up at the window The the average customer will pay two dollars and nine cents on a hamburger Okay, so that's in like a very good profit margin they spent a dollar 91 They made two dollars so so McDonald's is making a whopping like what's that 20 cents 20 whatever that the math is on that Okay, 18 cents even even worse. They're not making 18 cents for every single person they get to the drive-through You're like man that Ronald McDonald is not that smart. He's a clown. He hasn't figured this out yet But Ronald McDonald is actually very smart, right? Okay, someone comes through he they spend the dollar 91 in ads they get some come through order hamburger They make their 18 cents, but then what do they say? Can you try upsell you fries in a coke? You want to supersize that? I don't think I say supersize that anymore, but if you look at McDonald's growth when they added in the supersize upsell I said hey, do you want supersize this boom their company took off they explode like never before and so they added fries and coke for $1 77 more and guess what happened? Okay from that dollar 77 they got to make and keep a dollar 32 profit Okay eight times the profit by adding in the back end Okay, now a lot of your companies right now if you're struggling I'm guessing you're missing part of it You're either missing a front end or a back end or something in between and so that's what we're gonna talk about right now It's a concept that we call the value ladder, okay, and this is what a value ladder kind of looks like Okay, you know so on the left-hand side We got value and the the error goes up so so the more value provide the higher goes here and the bottom side We got price okay, and so as we move this direction price gets more and more expensive now for me If I was if someone was coming to say Russell, what's the best thing you have to offer? It's always gonna be back here at the top of the value ladder Okay, they're gonna receive the most amount of value possible and it's gonna be the most expensive thing possible Okay for us we think that we think the most value I can possibly provide some names right now Okay, so for me, it's a program that we actually launched recently and it was almost as a joke initially I didn't know if anyone was gonna buy it, but we launched it and we recently signed up our very first customer and it's a million dollar package Okay, what the way it works they pace a hundred thousand dollars me and my team my team and I I'm not sure we say that we fly out to their office and We set up their offer We set up a mini call center for them and we set up their traffic generation systems We drive traffic's a hundred thousand dollars up front plus 10% of the profits up to ten million dollars at that point We have made a million dollars and then the contract ends so it's a million dollar package That's the best value I could ever provide somebody okay by far hands down That's what how I can serve my customers at the highest level now if I was walking up to you on the street I was like hey great to meet you man. I See you got a business here your chiropractor. It's awesome. What I want to do is if you give me a million dollars I'll take your business the next level you in You're gonna say You can say no Russell you crazy. I'm not gonna give you a million dollars You're there's no way you're that cool or even close to that cool right? Okay, and then why is that it's because that point I provide a no value They don't know if I'm good or if I'm bad or anything So what we do in the business we start down here at the bottom left hand Okay, we provide a little bit of value at a little bit of cost because because people will test something right to test the waters and see if They like something they'll try it out Okay, this is and this is comes back to what we'll talk about here in a minute is our bait right But we're testing out some we're putting some bait out there in the water and someone's gonna take it Okay, for those you guys who came through any of our funnels it might have been the dot-com secrets labs book might have been another product But there's there's some bait we put out there right and if you get that it's not gonna cost you very much But if you get that and you like it and you receive value what naturally happens next You're like man, that was awesome. I receive value wasn't that expensive? What else do they have and naturally if they receive value at this level? They're gonna naturally want to send up to the next level Okay, and here now you can enter into it something you sell them something else and they receive value at this level Guess what's gonna happen? They're gonna ascend up to the next level and the next level and the next level Okay, and that's how a value ladder works and all has to do with how much value you can provide somebody Okay, so for me I come in and we give away our free dot-com secrets labs books I'm a ghost to that experience they they get a bunch stuff and they want the next thing and then the next thing the next thing and eventually we close people on our our 8000 our coaching our 25,000 our million dollar coaching and they move up through a value ladder Okay, and that's kind of how this thing works, but it all has to do 100% with how much value you provide people Okay, if you're screwing people which happens so much on the internet Guess what you may get one one shot yet one transaction might get somebody but that it'll end there But if you're providing real legitimate value people buy from you for me for years and years and years and years And that's the goal of building a value ladder the right way Okay, now on the bottom-hand side here you notice there's there's a dollar signs going across this way This is your continuity program because any good business you got to have a value ladder We also need to have continuity where people are paying you monthly for for access to software for for something and we'll talk about how to how to create this later on in the presentation But just so you understand from this diagram what that is All right now I want to show you guys a value ladder for a dentist and I had an experience a couple years ago Excuse me that kind of illustrated this really really well, so when I first started my business I We got started and we didn't It was just me at first and then my my wife and I and then we start hiring some employees And after we had four or five employees They all start asking us if we had benefits and I had never had a job before I didn't know what benefits meant Turns out benefits means you pay for people's insurance and their dental insurance all this stuff We never done that before but I was like, you know what? Everyone's asking for so let's give everyone benefits We created these benefit package and so for the first time in like six years I had I had dental insurance which was kind of exciting and a little while later I got a postcard in the mail that said Come in from from a dentist that said come in for a free teeth cleaning. I was like sweet I got my insurance now I got I got this teeth cleaning and and the bait was set right the company put out some bait and I took it and I Called the company and said yes only my teeth clean for free. They had me come in Came in I met the dentist met the assistants met everyone they sat me down They cleaned my teeth for free and it was awesome. I was super excited and while they're in there cleaning my teeth The dentist says hey, I just had a question for you Do you smoke or do you drink coffee or anything? I was like what what are you talking about? So yeah, I noticed your teeth are kind of yellow I just I wasn't sure if it's because your smoker or drink coffee or what? I was like my teeth are yellow. Are you serious? He said yeah, well, they are but I'm like, no I don't drink or smoke or anything like that. He's like, oh, that's wow I feel kind of awkward now and I was like, well, what can you do about it? I don't want I don't want yellow teeth if you're noticing I'm sure everyone else is too and he said well don't worry We have these we have these whitening kits and we'll give you gel and we'll give you all this stuff to whiten your teeth I'm like, yes, please give me that and and like a good marketer did he provided more value Because that he was able to sell me the next thing on his value ladder Excuse me, so then a few minutes later. He's going through and he said hey, so did you ever have braces? I said yeah, how can you tell I said well your teeth are kind of crowding and that happens a lot of times when people had braces when They were young so my teeth are crowding. He said yeah But if you want we can create a retainer and the retainer will keep your teeth in place and that way It won't look like you your teeth are all jacked up I'm like, oh, yeah, are you serious like my my teeth are messed up and he said well if you want me to make a retainer I will I said yes, please make me a retainer and and That's where the value ladder ended for me. I had my teeth have been really messed up. I'm sure the next episode of said hey Russell, I'm sure you know we can do this we can do and the next episode would have been cosmetic dentistry Whatever it else it is, okay, and then when I was leaving my my checkup that day they the Assistant said hey, we'll sign you up for six months from now to come back and boom They signed me up for their for their continuity program. Okay, so what just happened here? I came in a dentist put out some really good bait. It was a free teeth cleaning I came in and I received value my teeth are getting clean is awesome And then they figured out how can they provide more value to me and boom I I wanted to get my teeth wide and they provided more value and I kept ascending up this value ladder I naturally wanted to ascend because I received value at each point and I walked in on a free teeth cleaning and I left with a Two or three or four thousand dollar bill. I don't remember what it was And so that's how a value ladder works. Okay, I'm sure you guys have all been to the dentist before you've seen this before But now you'll probably respect your dentist more when you see that man. They understand how to do this Okay and for me it was kind of sad because My wife went in two days later with her free postcard teeth cleaning and she walked away with whitening trays and a retainer as well So they got both of us and that postcard campaign probably made him eight or nine thousand bucks And all said and done and then we've been clients now for them for almost ten years So it works you guys it works all day long. All right, so here's the value ladder for my company Just so I can be completely transparent you guys will see this and it'll uh, it'll it'll make a lot of sense Okay, so the front of my value ladder usually some kind of free quiz or squeeze page or something I'm trying to get somebody in the front door after that we give away a free book free plus shipping Okay, I'm guessing most you guys on this webinar probably Got this book next we have our invisible funnel webinar Okay for all you guys from this webinar right now, this is where you are in my value ladder Okay, you are currently on an invisible funnel style webinar where I'm training you for free and providing as much value as possible For two reasons first off I want to provide as much value as possible second off if you like this I'm hoping you're gonna send up either my home study course when my events or minor circle There's my value ladder Okay, we do it all day long and then when you go through this process My goal is to get you on my continuity program, which is a software product called click funnels I mean sign up for that I get paid every single month Okay, boom. There's full transparency. There is my doc com secrets company Okay, I guess showing my value ladder for everyone my companies when the first things we map out when we come into a company because The problem that most people have is they come in they start a business and they've got one of these things They don't have all they got one maybe two and they struggle a good example That is one of my buddies is a chiropractor and I'm sure I hope we got a lot of chiropractors on this webinar right now But typically most chiropractors they live and they die on their 40 or $50 adjustment Okay, so one of my friends is a chiropractor. He started this new clinic and And he started doing this he started getting clients and bringing him in doing the adjustment And he'd make 40 or 50 bucks every time you get an adjustment boom And then the continuity for them is keep coming in a couple times a week for adjustments Okay, so he's got two pieces of this value ladder built out and one day it called me and said hey Russell You're this great marketing guru give me some magic so I can grow my grow my practice And we started talking a little bit and I was like well, you know like tell me about your business and you explained it And I was like so all you do is just adjustments. Yeah, I'm like what else do you provide people? He's like, that's it That's what we do and I was like, huh, it's just that's kind of interesting Let me think about it a little bit and I'll give back to you and so literally like a week later Some of you guys know my background I was a wrestler in high school and in college and a couple years ago I started this wrestling club and I and I actually flew out the entire I had the entire Olympic Greco wrestling team moved to Boise and we build the whole training center and it was it was really a really fun project for a couple years And we had all these guys training here for the Olympics And we had a chiropractor who's coming in a couple times a week to adjust the guys and one day after practice the chiropractor called And she wasn't able to show up but all the guys were like man our spines hurt our neck hurts You know, I mean we're throwing each other around just just in a pretty bad spot and so And so they they needed to be to be adjusted and one of the wrestlers was intelligent guy He said, you know what I can figure this out and he went on YouTube and he typed in chiropractic adjustment and within five minutes He watched someone explain how to do it He went into the room and he adjusted every single person on the team and I wasn't there that day But he called me afterwards and told me said hey I'm a chiropractor. I adjusted everyone on the team and he told me the kind of the process of the story and I started laughing so Hard I said hey, I got to call someone I'll call you back So I called my buddy who's who's an a chiropractor and I called him up and I was like hey man. Just so you know My friend and he knew the guys well I said he was able to to to replicate every single thing you do in your business And he learned it in five minutes on YouTube and I kind of I kind of I kind of waited and what happened next was really funny My buddy the chiropractor he obviously got upset Okay, he'd gone to who knows how many years of school to become a chiropractor and he came on He's like he's like you can't just do that. You can't do today He's like you're minimalizing what we do is a profession this and this and I'm letting him kind of rant for a minute I got a big smile on my face and I stopped I said wait I said if I was calling just make fun of you, I probably wouldn't have called you But there's there's a lesson behind this I said What else did you learn while you were medical school? Okay, we're gonna start talking about where do we find and how do we communicate with our dream customers? Okay, you understand this stuff. This is where I Really think it starts becoming magical when you start understanding these pieces of it Okay, so we're gonna jump right in so the first thing I want you to understand again is there's three types of traffic Okay, and something that I wish more people will talk about one day I was sitting there Excuse me, I was focusing on a presentation for some some of our Our inner circle members. I was thinking about just conceptually this whole business I was trying to figure out how to make it simple how to make it simple and all of a sudden I realized that there are three types of traffic and with that there are three types of websites I said when you understand that it suddenly becomes really really easy To get traffic. So let me show you how this works. Okay, three types of traffic first type of traffic is traffic that you control Okay, second type is traffic that you don't control And the third is traffic that you own And I'm gonna say a couple things here That we're gonna kind of go over really really Really fast. I'm gonna come back over and go through it a couple times It'll make perfect sense to you by the time we get here. Okay, so the first thing what you understand before we Dive too much into that is you have to understand how how traffic on the internet kind of works. Okay, and this is what When I when I first got this it's it's what made this whole internet marketing thing just makes sense to me. Okay, I Start thinking about about just the fact that that people online they congregate together based on similar beliefs and values Okay, so when I think about The the word congregation, what do you typically think of when you guys hear the word congregation? Okay, for most of you guys yeah, most of you are saying right now, so you hear what here word congregation you think of a church, right? Churches they have congregations of people who who congregate together Based on similar beliefs and similar values am I right and so you have to understand that and so when we know that if We know that all of the all of the Baptists are congregating together in one central location Okay, all of the the Mormons are congregating together all the Catholics are congregating together Okay, they're they're self congregating themselves together and after we know that if I was selling something to Mormons Where would I go? I'd go to the Mormon church because every single week all of them congregate together and they're hanging out and it's literally like Like putting your fishing pole into water and pulling out who you want Okay, now I'm not telling you guys now or ever to sell things to churches But what I want to show you is when we talked about This earlier Talk about where this is the key guys when you understand this where this is if you look at traffic All right, okay, everyone's always figuring out how do I get traffic? I don't get traffic and they think it's like this magical thing, but traffic is not magical This is what happens online people they congregate together based on similar similar beliefs and values Okay, when I was wrestling I wasn't and I was in college We had this mandatory study hall every single night because they I guess they assume the wrestlers were dumb and that we weren't gonna do our homework and It was actually very true But all of us had to do two hours of mandatory study hall every single night So we all go to study hall and there were computers and all of us would jump on the computers and every single one of us Every night we get on one website. We'd go to the mat.com We'd sit there and we jump on the message boards and we talk about wrestling for two hours every single night And guess what what's interesting that was happening at every single college around the country and around the world And every single high school as well Everyone who is anyone who cared about wrestling they were all congregating themselves together on one central website Okay, now me as a marketer if I was going after wrestlers, where would I throw my my bait? Okay, I'd go to the mat.com. I'd be buying banner ads and buying email ads I'd be doing everything I can to fish people out of that pond because that's where they're congregating Okay, whatever business you're in right now. I promise you people are congregating just they're congregating and so for us We get to be out where are those people congregating? What websites are they on? What forms they read? What on Facebook? What groups are they in? What keywords they searching for in Google? What are the what are the where those people out? What email newsletters? They read what blogs they are because if I again because I if I know exactly who my target market is And I can figure out exactly where they're where they're congregating Traffic becomes really easy you guys All I gotta do is I gotta find what they're at and throw my bait in the pond Buy a banner ad send an email out to the customer list Okay, throw some bait out and boom I got him and I can take more wanted to go That make a sense you guys So that's that's the key to traffic. We understand that traffic becomes very Very easy. Okay. Now like I said, there's three types of traffic traffic you control traffic You don't control and traffic you own so traffic that you control This is the this is like I go like I don't own the traffic at Google, right? Google owns that so I don't own it, but I can control it I can go to Google I can sign up for an account and Google let me pay them to be able to control that traffic I can say hey Google here's a dollar for every single person that clicks on my ad when they click on it Send them over here Send me with Facebook go to Facebook. Hey Facebook. You got a ton of people here Here's a dollar every time someone clicks put them over here to my website Okay, I can buy ads and people's email newsletters like my banner at same constant. I don't own that traffic Okay, Google or Facebook or the even person who owns the email list or the blog they own that traffic But I can come I can control that by buying ads on those platforms Okay, that's traffic. I control now anytime the traffic I control I always send traffic I control some kind of squeeze page. Okay, we'll talk about here in a minute, but all traffic I control needs to go to a squeeze page Okay, second type of traffic is traffic that I don't control Kids the traffic that just shows up on a website randomly like someone on social media says something about me and all of a sudden people Start coming to a website and I didn't control that they just show up Okay, or somebody goes into Google to type in something weird and all and my website pops up on the top 10 results And they just show up. Okay. Good example. That is a couple of years ago Somebody wrote an article about me and they said Russell Brunson is a little snot I took the article I posted on my blog and a couple days later One of my SEO guys came into the office laughing and giggling and said hey go to Google and type in little snot And guess what at the time? I don't know if I still have not but if you typed in little snot into Google guess who's number one in Google for a little snot It was me. Okay. Now, obviously that was not very targeted But what's interesting is that if somebody was to go to Google type in little snot and they saw I was ranked number one They clicked on that somehow they show up on my website and I didn't control that person They just randomly showed up. I don't know how they got there. They just did okay So anytime anything search engine related is traffic. I don't control it's anything like press Okay, if I get on TV or a blog or writes about me or something something like that I get a ton of people to my website tons of traffic, but I don't know how they got there I don't control them. I can't create that and you can do things to manipulate it, but I don't I don't control that I don't control it just kind of happens. So for traffic. I don't control I usually send them to my blog and on my blog you'll notice that I've got a goal as my goal is to get their email address Okay Now the third type of traffic is the best. This is traffic that I own Okay, for me right now I have an email list of over a half a million people who have subscribed my email list and I own that traffic Every day I wake up and I say where should I send this traffic? I said I want to send it to this website over here I can write a little email a click send and boom instantly 500,000 people get a message from me and I'll get Thousands and thousands of clicks and that traffic goes to wherever I want because I own that traffic That's the best kind of traffic that traffic is free the other kind cost money your traffic you control caught I got to pay Google or Facebook every time someone clicks on an ad traffic. I don't control It just magically shows up You can pay people to SEO and write press releases and do stuff, but it's not it's just not something I can rely on Traffic I own is the best so when I know that my goal as a marketer Someone who's driving traffic is to convert all traffic that I control and all traffic I don't control into traffic that I own Okay, the way I do that is I drive these people through a little website that we call a squeeze page Come this page. They put their email address in they click submit and what happens that person's added to my email list Boom now. It's traffic that I own. It's the best Okay, someone someone writes a blog post about me. They go to dot com secrets dot com They show up on my page they they see at the very top if you notice any of my blogs the top one third My my entire goal is to get their name in their email address They cut my site they put an email address and boom all of a sudden guess what now I own that traffic Okay, guys, because this is the best so I'm learning about traffic I got to find out where those people are at I got I got to convert those people from traffic that I that I control and I don't control into traffic that I own Because when I own that traffic man, you guys that's when this whole internet marketing game becomes it becomes amazing Okay, I first learned that when I was getting started. I remember That's probably over 10 years ago now I was reading an article of this guy and he was talking about he said I'm sure you've seen online all these these these gurus talking About how they made 50 or a hundred thousand dollars in a day or a million dollars in a day He says you probably thought those things were scams, right and me as a 21 22 year old kid I was like, yeah, there's no way that that's not even the reality people I know make $20,000 a year let alone in a day Okay, and so I kind of I was reading the article and totally just just caught me He said no the realities those things are true you guys said this is how it works He says that a person a guru or whatever starts building an email list and let's say first they build a list of a thousand people Then ten thousand then a hundred thousand then a million Okay, let's just say let's say you have a list of a hundred thousand people You send an email out to that list of a hundred thousand people and you get just one percent of those people to open Okay, that's what ten thousand opens and you get one percent of people to buy That's Man of Horrible math. What's that a hundred hundred buy hundred purchases and they're buying a 50 dollar product What do you just make you say 50 or you made $5,000 by sending an email to your list? That was in one day. Okay one of the metrics we try to base our business off of is we want to make one dollar Per month per name on my email address Okay, it kind of stays similar to that when I when I had a list of about a hundred people I was making about a hundred bucks a month when I had a list of a thousand people I was making about thousand bucks a month. Well, this was about ten thousand people I was making about ten thousand dollars a month hit a hundred thousand I was a hundred thousand a month hit five hundred thousand and it keeps going on from there So those are some of the metrics you can look at as you're building your list To know if you're if you're kind of on track if you're doing it, right? I want to show you guys how to reverse engineer a Funnel okay when I first got into this business one of my one of my early mentors was a guy named Stephen Pierce and Stephen was a Stephen's a smart guy and I remember he told me one day he said he said you know Russell people always ask me like How do you how do you create traffic? He said you don't have to create traffic traffic's already there You said figure out to get your message out in front of it. That's why I started thinking about that. I said man That's true. So there's all this traffic already happening I got my message in front of it, but who are the people that are already making money there like who's already in that river of traffic Who's already in that pond making money? How are they doing it? I want to find out exactly what they're doing so if I can figure out what they're doing I can reverse engineer that and get as close to possible as close to what they're doing as possible. Okay, so We started going through and started figuring this out So this is the the process we go through when we're trying to figure out where traffic sad and what people are doing that already works Okay, I promise you as if you do this exercise and I'm gonna show you guys You will be a thousand times closer than being successful than most people who get started Okay, so starts over here with with the where okay, where are your people at now? You're gonna find there's direct competitors and then there's indirect competitors Okay, direct competitor somebody who's selling the exact same thing as you okay I got a weight loss product boom There's a guy selling weight loss product gets a direct competitor Okay, indirect competitor is I got a weight loss product. There's the guy selling a juicing product Similar audience not a direct competitor. Maybe I can buy ads over there. So looking for these traffic streams That's why we designed this look like little little streams, right? So my direct traffic stream and my indirect traffic stream. Okay, so where are those things at? Next thing to find out is is these these five elements Okay after I know where other people are at my competitors are at what are they doing? Okay? Who are the demographics? They're targeting What's their offer? What's their landing page look like? Where are the traffic sources? What ad copy is working for them? I need to know what these five things are and after I know these five things are I'm gonna model what works I'm gonna find out what's working. I'm gonna model it Okay, the worst thing you could do is go into a new business and try to just to make something up from scratch Model what's already working? I learned this from Tony Robbins. He talked about like everything he's wanted to do his life He's like I found the best person in the world. I looked at him I model him and then I was successful very very quickly Okay, guys coaching students who that's one of the main reasons that why they join our coaching rustly are successful You've proven it. I want to model you I'm gonna get in the highest level and just figure out how to do exactly what you're doing Right, so they model it works. Okay, so that's kind of that's kind of how this works All right next we're shifting to you guys now now we showed kind of Where where we can find our cuss our audience? The second part of the section is how do we communicate with that audience? Okay, and we communicate through a concept I call the attractive character now I initially learned this whole concept from one of my friends who's in the dating market his name is John all in us and John teaches men how to get women to approach them Okay, now I don't know about you guys, but for most men that's like the dream offer, right? I Don't want to talk to girls. I'm scared of them want girls to come to me And that's what he teaches and that's what he kind of coaches on is is that about how to do that So anyway, I heard him speak one time in a marketing seminar I thought he was awesome had him come speak one of my events and in my event He started talking about this concept called the attractive character and I still remember like it was yesterday He was in front of the audience and he said look you guys are selling all sorts of stuff I'm selling a bunch of different stuff. He's like what I found is that He said that That the law of attraction is the same whether you're trying to get people To like if you're if you're one of my guys on my list who are trying to get women to approach them Or if you're in business, you're trying to get customers to come to you the laws of attraction are the same He says that we start that process by creating an attractive character and remember we start talking about this I had some some big a ha's and it just kind of opened my mind and This is kind of the the core concept behind the attractive character I'm gonna break all the stuff down for you here in a minute But the first one kind of just give you guys some ideas about how this attractive character works So good example, this is one of my buddies. His name is Jacob Hiller and so he has made no Jacob He is literally one of my favorite people on earth. He's really cool. And so Jacob likes to jump Okay, that's one of his favorite things. He jumps like crazy and and what he was in college He just liked it so much he started making videos and posting on YouTube about how he liked jumping and teach people how he jumped and things to increase your vertical and all sorts of stuff, right and He started making all his YouTube videos and guess what happened. He was so passionate excited about jumping He started getting this huge audience of people who were interested and they started following him He started building up his platform You know, I don't know if Jacob was doing this intentionally. I don't think he was I think he was just passionate about a topic He started just putting value out there in the world. Okay, and for most of you guys depending where you are in your business This is a great place to start Pick your passion to start putting it out there giving away giving away making videos or blogging or whatever platform You're the most comfortable putting it out there for him. It was YouTube He's just jumping all these videos and people started loving him He started getting a thousand followers then ten thousand they kept growing from there and eventually all these people just loved him he built this attractive character and And everyone loved what he was teaching and somewhere along the line I don't know if somebody told them or if he had the idea of he thought you know I could probably sell these guys something that they love everything. I'm selling let me create a product So he created a product called the jump manual that teaches people how to increase their vertical and He went out to this audience. He already created this platform. He'd already built any basics and hey guys Here's the book and now he sells For I don't know probably five or six years in a row makes over a thousand dollars a day consistently selling the jump manual Okay, pretty exciting don't you think? You know, it's cool about Jacob and his wife and his little family here is home and their story is just awesome But they have not had a home for like six years I think when they first launched this business They decided to travel and so they packed up all their stuff and they went to a country for three months And then they're making tons of money out there just doing their thing and and then they decided let's go to another country and they they packed up their stuff went to another country and every three months or so they moved to a new country and they just live out of their suitcases and They literally do that because they're the website business and making a thousand bucks a day or more And and that's kind of what he does. So it's pretty exciting Definitely someone who who kind of figured out this whole lifestyle business we talked about right? Okay, so Jacob Hillers has stud next one's guy I'm sure a lot of you guys have heard about he's written three New York Times bestseller books now Got him Gary Vaynerchuk Now Gary's dad his family owned a wine business at the time they're doing about three million bucks a year Gary came on he became the attractive character of this company tons of passion loved wine I love talking about it launched a company or a website called wine library TV We talked about wine reviewed wines and every single day for like six years He made videos about wine and just talked about passionate built a huge following of people Millions of people who watch these videos and took his company from doing three million dollars a year to 45 million dollars a year By bringing out this attractive character and getting an audience to follow him Pretty exciting and all he did is by sharing his passion for wine. Okay Now a lot of people say well Russell this this is makes sense for like guru type businesses about other companies Well, this is these are two different software companies that are huge companies kiss metrics and crazy egg both these companies do well, but both these companies are fueled by and and and And the traffic is driven not so much traffic driven but the the companies are fueled by the attractive character this guy named Neil Patel and Neil is a Seriously one of my favorite marketers online They just a brilliant guy, but he blogs a blogging is his platform. They listen to him and boom they start buying his stuff Okay Fast food companies. Okay fast subway was a fast food company like every other company And I don't think they're doing that well and then like I don't know 10 or 15 years ago They found this dude who was severely overweight ate nothing but subway for two years lost a ton of weight and Jared became the attractive character for subway and literally transformed them from a fast food company to a weight loss plan You look at their look at their numbers look what happened to them after they transformed and it was crazy Okay, Jared became this attractive character They put this character out there and people who were wrong lose weight looked at Jared and said man If he can lose that much weight by going to subway I can do it too and suddenly people relate to that attractive character They start following and they start doing what he says right and that's how subway became a weight loss plan Okay, think about movies what movies are popular Okay, every movie usually has one or two a level celebrity an attractive character that That people are gonna be attracted to a lot of times people have no idea what the movies even called like hey You're going to the new the new Tom Cruise movie. I think they I was reading the thing the other day about flow She's like the highest paid spokesperson in the world Making millions and millions dollars a year and she's just attractive character some people haters people love her But man, she's getting people to buy insurance same with this little get go dude, right? Okay, he goes out there. He's controversial. He's annoying. He's whatever you want to say But he gets this huge audience of people. He's an attractive character He's got millions and millions and millions of listeners and all of a sudden the eyeballs are there and Advertisers come to him. Okay, he can literally sell anything on I've got people who sell business books buying ads on his Radio station I got people selling all sorts of stuff because he's got an audience of people there and because there's a audience because there's an attractive character Because people are shown up and listening and paying attention. He can sell anything Okay, think about Oprah think about Ellen now think about the talk shows they build an audience through an attractive character and then they can sell anything Okay, so for you guys that's where I think most businesses need to start is What's your attractive character? Who's the person communicating with the audience? Who's the person that people are gonna be attracted to they're gonna listen to and they're gonna want to follow Ken look at my business. Okay, this is You know for years I've been sharing my attractive character with people and I'm not that good looking I apologize but I find things in my life that that That people relate to and I share them when I first got started in this business It was my wife and I just got married and I was wrestling I used to talk about wrestling all the time I'd write emails about it. I blog about all this is before blogs actually But I would I would write stuff about it and guess what people who were athletes started buying my products I would go to a seminar I would speak and I'd sell at the end of the seminar look in the back of the room and there'd be a whole bunch of athletes back there They'd be like hey, man, I love your stuff. Just bought your package. By the way, I used to play football Hey, by the way, I used to be a volleyball player and the athletes were attracted to me because outside I would share with people a couple years later my wife and I Try we're trying to get pregnant and we couldn't and we went through this big process and finally we were able to get pregnant with Twins and I didn't talk about that for about a year or so And remember one time I was an event and I started talking and I was talking about why I was passionate about business And just out of the blue I started sharing the story about my wife and I and how we got pregnant and how how she had found The fertility doctor on Oprah and all this stuff and how grateful I was that that company was willing to go out there market Their business and how it affected my life and changed changed our life and I shared that I looked out in the audience People were crying and I went and sold my thing at the end in the back of the room Guess what the athletes were still there, but now there were There are all these other people that showed up there were wives and mothers and Grandparents who started buying my stuff who never bought it before because I started sharing that aspect of my attractive character So we start putting things out there you guys different people will be attracted to that and they will buy because of that Okay, I was in the seminar one time and and I was speaking and I was selling this product on on Like how to make money building a list or email stuff and I sold the end of it I did great. We made a ton of money and right after me a Lady some of you guys know her Ali Brown. She spoke right after me And Ali is is awesome and she got up and she gave almost the same presentation to me I felt I remember sitting the audience feeling bad I'm like I can't believe that they had her speak right after me I was talking about this building she was talking about this building and she did a thing and she pitched And I was like this is gonna be so embarrassing like everybody bought my product and she did her pitch and guess what happened All women popped up out of the room ran to the back and they bought it hers was more expensive And she sold more she made more money than me at that event because she she attracted different audience an audience Who's willing to spend more than my audience apparently and so the attractive character thing is big you guys all you guys need to figure out What the attractive character is in your business and then share it? All right, so here is the the worksheet that kind of goes with the attractive character Excuse me and so as you're starting to kind of design yours here all the pieces that are that are evolved Okay, first over here we start with the elements the attractive character then the identities and then the storylines Now I literally can do it a full day-long event just on this concept But obviously we don't have that much time so I'm gonna go to kind of go through this fast But these are all the the keys of the attractive character. Okay, so first key is every good attractive character has a backstory Okay, what is your backstory? How did you get to where you are today right now? Okay, you've got to shut up with your audience because the backstory is where like let's say you're in the weight loss market If you used to be way overweight now you're skinny If you don't tell the backstory if you were always skinny and you tell people that they're like well You were always skinny you don't get it if you say hey, I was 300 pounds I struggled and then I came through this process now. I'm skinny You're gonna attract people and say man this person's been where I've been I can do that as well back stories huge Second is parables. Okay, it's key that when you are talking to your audience. You are sharing parables Okay, I've shared a ton of parables with you guys already in the last hour and a half that we've been that we've been on When I when I teach this in a in a full like three or four day event I share a lot more parables and they're just stories to help people to understand a point, right? I share my fertility story now to illustrate a point I share my potato gun story when I'm trying to teach people about information marketing I share different stories different parables to get people to understand core concepts I'm teaching and making very simple and easy to understand. Okay, it's for all you guys You have to know what parables do you have in your life that you can share with your audience? It gets them closer to you gets them more likely to buy from you Okay, number three is character flaws This one's gonna be very hard for most of you guys to share your character flaws But one of the best things you can do is share character flaws with people and they see that you have flaws They're gonna be more likely to listen to you to respond to you and want to get closer to you. Okay character flaws are huge You think about like the great superheroes in life look at he man Superman Batman like what's interesting about them is not how perfect They are what's interesting is the character flaws. Okay, Superman didn't have kryptonite. He wouldn't be interesting Okay, if Batman didn't have his family and his loved ones that that could that That kept him from doing everything, you know that they kept him Morally grounded he wouldn't be that interesting the character flaws will make people interesting So it's important to be sharing your character flaws. Okay, and then number four here is polarity The biggest problem a lot of people have with attractive characters that they're trying to be neutral I don't want to offend anybody and when you offend nobody you also don't excite anybody Okay, being neutral is the worst thing you could do online. You have to be polar. Okay, you have to have some polarity You think about someone like Howard Stern like he's built his business off a controversy It's the group people who hate him who just despise him who don't like him at all But there's also group people who love him. Okay, look at my business There are people if you type my name in a Google there are people who do not like me Okay, because I'm I have some polarity if I was appealing to everybody all the time, you know it it I mean it's boring. Nobody's gonna listen to you But as soon as you you have some polarity and you have some stamp you take stances on things and you push the envelope a little bit That's when you you'll see this this this polar opposite happen where a percentage people start not like you But a bigger percent will like you and so people like for you will start buying from you and and That's really the key polarity is huge. Okay, so those are the core elements of the attractive character next over your way of the identity So each attractive character is gonna have one identity Okay Let's see so some of you guys and again one of these is gonna fit for each of you some of you guys are gonna be the leader okay an example of the of a Like a leader attractive characters like Donald Trump, right? I'm the leader. He didn't want to do it. It wasn't his goal but he feels he feels drawn to that because Because it's his mission. It's what he needs to do. Okay, if you look at the storylines I share with people it's typically more of that thing like I don't I didn't sign up one day to become a Marketing guru and to teach things to people but man, there were people getting screwed out there and people teaching bad information we built companies we had success and and You know because of that I came out we started teaching stuff like and I reluctantly became this became the hero So reluctant hero is my personal favorite But for each of you guys your personality types gonna fit one of these you have to figure out which one that is and then embrace it And you can change throughout your your business and throughout your life initially we got started I was the reporter I was interviewing people like crazy because I didn't know anything And that's how I learned a lot of this stuff after I had kind of done that Then I shipped them or into like the reluctant hero or more the leader just kind of shifting my identity So you had to pick which one makes the most sense for you and then run with it Okay, and then as you're as the attractive character you're gonna be communicating with your audience through storylines Okay, and we're gonna talk a lot more about story here in a little bit, but these are six core storylines we use a lot First is loss and redemption Oh, wait, let me see my notes here. We're gonna put this into Into some framework. Okay, so somebody comes in they just joined my squeeze page. They're on my list now What's the how do I introduce them to my attractive character? How am I building that relationship? How am I getting them to want to ascend up to my to my higher levels? Can we do that through the two processes one's called a soap opera sequence and one's called a daily sign-filled email? Now I learned these two concepts. I learned soap opera sequence from a guy named Andre Chaperone I learned the daily sign-filled emails from a guy named Ben Settle. What's cool is I went to one of their mastermind groups They're both there teaching their email marketing philosophies and they were sitting there arguing back and forth on who was right if the soap opera Is right if the sign-filled is right and I sat back listening to these guys and I kind of stop and say you guys I don't think you understand how brilliant both of you are and if you use these two pieces in conjunction It can do some amazing things. I like doing the soap opera initially because when a new person comes into my list They're brand new. They don't know who I am yet They just opted in for some freebie or something like that. I'm taking through this process now I need to I need to warm them up to my attractive character I need to build this relationship as quick as possible and I do that through my soap opera sequence now that I've got them through My soap opera sequence my goal is to send them up my value ladder and do that through my daily sign-filled emails Okay, so let me break down how this kind of works First is the soap opera sequence. Okay now if you look at guys like Andre Chaperone He literally write soap opera sequences that are six months long I don't know how he does it He's intense and he's got all these hooks and these angles and he calls it So popper sequences because literally what he does is it just like a good soap opera, right? It it opens all these loops in each episode that then close and then in the you know Tomorrow's episode but during tomorrow's episode opens up like 10 new loops then close in the next day And so they run these things for like, I don't know soap operas and running probably for 40 years with the same storyline because they never they never let anything close, right? And that's why people watch these things every day because because how good they are opening and closing loops, right? So that's kind of how soap opera sequence works is is I'm gonna have these emails going out that are opening loops and getting people interested and I'm trying to hook them in but my my main goal I got two goals personally My first goal is I want to build a relationship with the attractive character. Okay, and so for me I do this through a five-day email sequence And you can make it longer you make it shorter, but for me This is how I do it five days and usually that that soap opera sequence is all It's building a relationship with the attractive character and pushing him back to my first offer the first thing I'm trying to sell them Okay, and then and then after that shifts the sign felt but we'll go through this first Okay, so soap opera so day number one when somebody first joins my list the first email I get kind of sets the stage Okay, so these emails typically saying hey, thanks so much. My name is Russell. Thank you for joining my list over the next Seven days. I'm gonna show you through my best strategies blah blah blah blah. It's gonna be awesome In fact tomorrow, I'm gonna and then I open my first loop tomorrow I'm gonna show you blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah Right, so check my email tomorrow. We're gonna and we'll dig deeper then okay So boom there's email number one setting the stage for the relationship What's gonna be happening what they can expect and things like that? Okay, now day number two My email is gonna start in a point of high drama. Okay, if you look at good storytelling I learned this initially from one of my friends and business partners Dagen Smith and Dagen Spent like two years studying story and how you can use story to sell things and one thing He told me that was just I thought the coolest thing in the world. He said if you look at any good story He said the story always starts at a point of high drama He says the problem is that most of us when we tell stories we start at the beginning of a story He said the problem at the beginning of story is not exciting in the middle is where he gets really really exciting So said what you got to do is you have to start a story at the point of high drama Okay, if you think about think about the good movies like Like well, I mean any of the good ones right cliffhangers one that pops in my head I remember I was like a 10 year old kid when that came out and I snuck over friends house and they were watching it I remember it was it was radar so I wasn't supposed to watch it and And my friend put it in the very first all I remember is like There's a vest just alone and all these people there They got this huge canyon and they're going across on this little zip wire They start going across and halfway through like someone's line breaks and they almost fall and they catch them They're trying to save them and all like this high drama and I remember sitting there and within like three minutes Look down my hands were soaking wet like I was just I got no any of the characters names I know anything all I knew is that it sucked me as this point of high drama And I was like oh and then it cut and then it goes back to the back story tells how they kind of got there Right and that's the best way to tell and a story through email as well day number two I start at the point of high drama Okay, so my story look at in my story I want to tell these guys to build the relationship with the attractive character. What's the point of high drama and I share that first And then I go to the back story the back story I go You know I can't tell the back story which which goes until point right at this wall Which is the frustration that I that I had Which is usually the frustration that they have okay if I know that they they're Trying to learn how to lose weight. I want that too. I want that too Every little other product or service or thing they tried usually is very surface level.
