That's kind of the process that we go through so we're always taking these daily things and after we're done Then we publish them and push them out to the world Okay All right, so that's kind of how our email sequences work you guys get traffic comes into the squeeze page Boom we have the soap opera sequence to introduce them to the attractive character Then the sign filled email to send them up or to sell them our continuity or whatever other products and services We have we're sending these daily sign for the most to engage them in whatever Level of the value out of we're trying to take them to so this changes like people say well What do you promote to your sign for the emails depends on what I'm selling right now? What am I excited about? What's the new thing? What's the exciting thing? What's the hot off for the day? And that's we're gonna be selling them Okay, all right now here's a couple ninja tricks and then I could literally spend A week just teaching this part of it I'm not going to I'm gonna give you guys some tricks though because this is where some of the more advanced stuff like after you start Communicating the audience like what are the other ways to to do cool stuff and so This is a concept I call inception and I call inception because if you guys all seen the movie inception I hope if not, that's your homework assignment go rent the movie and watch it But it's about these guys who who go into dreams and they try to place ideas into people's minds And when that person has the idea they think it's their idea and then they're more likely to act upon it, right? And so I started looking at our sales processes and and most people and they sell stuff They're so blunt and just like hey buy my stuff And I started looking like if I can if I can change the way we sell where instead of me telling people They should buy my stuff if I can create a process and an environment and a language pattern That gets them to have this idea of man. I should buy Russell stuff if I can cause that inception in somebody's mind It's a thousand times more powerful Okay People they hate to be sold to but they love to buy stuff. Okay, you have to understand that So I don't want to sell them stuff I want I want to cause inception in their mind so that they are going to want to buy what I have to sell That makes sense you guys. I know it's kind of woo-woo at this point Bow dig deeper to some of the concepts because a lot of stuff you can do and I wish we could spend a week on this right now But this will give you guys some some some gold nuggets at least So first thing to understand you guys is the attention span of a human being in 19 in the 1980s was 20 minutes Okay, 20 minutes. You think about like what interruptions you had? Okay. Once a day the mailman showed up You know, maybe once or twice a week someone come knock on your door The phone ring maybe maybe two or three times a week Maybe once a day me twice a day if you were popular and that was the distractions that people had right what happens today Today's a little bit different right every three seconds. We're looking at our cell phones or Facebook our text messages our Twitter our emails like People are jumping from thing to thing to thing to thing to thing and literally in 20 minute period of time You've probably checked 80 different things. Okay, they did a study recently and the average Person right now today in our world guess how long their attention span is any guesses in the in the comments I Would have I would assume maybe two or three minutes That's kind of I was thinking when I was reading this article about this The average attention span of a human being today in today's world is exactly nine seconds Okay Now when you understand that this is one of the keys this whole inception stuff. I'm talking about most people when Like when you start learning sales and marketing At least when I did I started studying for some of the greats right the people who have been teaching marketing for for a hundred years And there's some amazing people that are that are taught marketing some amazing books and you'll hear all the famous marketers They're like hey you should go read Claude Hopkins and Jean Schwartz and all these people's names and you should their stuff is awesome But you have to understand that they were selling in a time when the average attention span was 20 minutes Today we're selling to a world where the tensions the average attention span is nine seconds If you don't understand that you don't understand the changes that have happened in our world today You're gonna get run over from people like me who understand that okay? So because of that we do everything different now. We have to create our offers differently. We communicate our messages differently You know all this edutainment stuff. I'm talking about like attractive character excitement storytelling fun That stuff is is necessary today because if you are bored you're gone Okay, people today don't watch the presidential debates the most important thing in the world Why because it's boring they want to go watch something on YouTube that's You know they're gonna capture their attention and keep it five seconds Okay, that's the world we're in right now And if you're not if you're not capturing some attention in that tiny period of time you've got you've lost them Okay, so it's key now There's a lot of different elements this whole this whole Inception stuff, but one of the key parts of it. This is from a book from Blair Warren. I've ever met the sky I really want to This is a Kindle book you can get for like three bucks. It's called the the one sentence persuasion course and It's 27 words to make the world do your bidding And when I read this I was just like that dude gets it he understands it perfectly So this is the one sentence persuasion course says people will do anything for those who encourage their dreams Justify that justify their failures Lie their fears Confirm their suspicions and help them throw rocks at their enemies Okay, there's a lot of power in that Okay Look at the emails that you said are the nice and I look at the sales videos the sales letters the webinars all type of things and See if you're hitting on these things Okay, people do anything for those who encourages their dreams. Are you encouraging your audience's dreams? Number two is justify their failures. Are you just fine their failures? Okay, yeah, you need to be It's not your fault You know, it's the bag the big bad gurus. It's the food industry. It's whatever is keeping them back. Okay, lie their fears confirm their suspicions and Helping the throw rocks their enemies So every time I write and copy or create a webinar or whatever I always look at this And like how can I weave these things into to my communication? This is one of the big core parts about inception Okay, all right. Here's the graph that goes with inception secrets So the top there is kind of that the the one sentence persuasion guide, right encourage their dreams Justify their failures lie their fears confirm their suspicions and throw rocks their enemies now when I'm creating any kind of sales message The first thing I'm looking at is every single person has like this primary question Okay, when it comes to your business now I first learned this from Tony Robbins and he shared a concept It was date with destiny program that he called the primary question He said that every single human has a primary question It's always going on their head over and over and over again It drives every single choice you make in life some people's primary questions. How can I do this better? Can you know those people because every single situation there in their life trying to figure out how to do it better? How can I run faster? How can I make this business grow? How can I do this? How can I do this and they have this primary question that's driving every single one of their choices? Okay, other people's primary question is why do I do everything wrong? Okay, that person drives their life. It makes decisions a lot different, right? Oh, why do I do everything wrong? Why do I do everything wrong? Why am I so dumb? Why can't it you know, whatever that thinks everyone's got this primary question now? We can go off on a whole tangent on that I'm not going to but I want to understand that primary question is key and it would be good for you to know your your core audience What their primary question is but more specifically when it relates to your product What is I believe this this also happens like a product or service level that people have this this this primary question and so Five or six years ago when we had we have a big huge call center with 60 sales people and we were selling Beginner internet marketers how to start a business online and I remember one day I went down to the down there I was listening to these phone calls and with like I said 60 full-time sales guys and I would say Every probably 10 or 15 minutes I would hear this this question come up where the people would people would say Hey, if Russell stuff is so good, why don't you just give it to me for free and I'll pay him back from the from the money I make I used to think that's dumb Why would they say that but they kept coming up and kept coming up and kept coming up over and over and over again And one day I had this epiphany. I said, you know what? That's their primary question. That's that's my audience's primary question So how do I address that? How do I how do I help them understand that? And so? We I wrote a sales letter about that time for a product called comm secrets X and in that in that video the Addressed that primary question. I said look all the other gurus out there keep talking about how good their products are and and And if they really are that good, why don't they just give you that product for free? And then if you make money then they'll take your money Okay, and I brought that question up and I said, you know it makes sense to me Does it make sense to you guys and the people that were listening were like yes, of course like that's what I'm in thinking That's the primary question that's been happening in my head I said look, I'm gonna be different than other gurus and I'm gonna give it to you for free You come in here try out for 30 days for free and if it works for you you can pay me if not then I don't want I don't deserve If I can't make you money, I don't deserve your money and we flipped it around like that and I I created my offer to match their primary question and We literally went from zero to eight thousand members in that membership site in a month Okay, because I hit the right primary question Okay, so think about that with your audience like what's the question they have and if I can communicate that very very quickly in the exact same words They're saying it in I can get them to move and do a lot of cool stuff Okay, now the primary question leads to some kind of epiphany right the epiphany that I had like I had this epiphany that That boom you needed this you needed that this this change And so I'm usually driving my primary question to some kind of epiphany And I'll show this a lot deeper in the sales scripts you guys here in a minute But just understand that the primary question is leaving some kind of epiphany which then leads to a magic bowl and that magic bowl Does your offer? right This is that this is that that thing you have that's gonna solve all their problems Back in the day we used to sell a lot more words like here's my thing And here's a million bullet points about why this thing is the greatest thing in the world Okay, and about six months ago. I had this I was working on a project We had a sales video that just wasn't converting and we were selling a product had six hours worth of event stuff Right it was six it was two three-day events and all the videos and it's like literally one of my favorite products I've ever created and I was selling it and in there I was saying sharing all like you get this and you get this and I was trying to share all the stuff that they got because I wanted them to know every single thing that that was possible in this in this program and And it just I we launched it twice in both times it did not convert And I was just like so frustrated like nice people understand what's in here This is like gold and one night I saw how I said what if what if I just find the best thing that I sell and just Only focus on that one thing like we cut out everything else and just focus on one thing the one thing And so I went through my entire six hours and I found one thing that was most interesting And I rewrote the entire sales message just to focus on that one thing the one magic bullet and literally in the six-day event I probably talked about that one thing for 30 minutes But that's all I talked about during my sales message is that one thing the one thing the one thing I kept going on going on Going on it and the end of my oh, yeah, you get a bunch of other stuff as well But this one thing is worth the price of admission and more and by changing it to be this this one thing This one focus one singular focus conversion went through the roof and so for most you guys Like how can you take your offer and really boil it down? It's like well the one thing What's the one thing that they really need if you can identify that and make it a tangible? They can see it and be like man. I need that thing that one thing It'll it'll do it'll do magic Okay, all right now a couple other things and again. I wish I had days to go into this and I don't I'm gonna go into really quickly one of them is copy speed and and typically um People write to people always writes. I wish I would have an example. I don't have an example here to show you guys right now but typically people would write sales letters and And when they do they I mean you've seen a sales letter there they're usually long blocky big paragraphs and things like that and a while ago we were doing some tests and I I was promoting some offers and testing it out and and there was this one offer that Just converted better than anything I'd ever seen before Before or since like it just did crazy stuff and and then we promoted and I went back and looked at it later And I realized it was just it was written differently was written In this weird like high-paced fast process. In fact, I wonder if I might have an example here Some of my notes I was looking through today Yeah, here it is so I was looking at this offer and I noticed that that the way was written was everything was very short Sentences with dot dots afterwards And there was never a sentence was more than like one line I thought that's really interesting And so I I met the guy who wrote this way and I tested some ads that he had written in every single one Was just the best company ads that I've ever done. I thought this is interesting So I wanted to kind of learn that style and to figure it out So and that's why I call this copy speed because this this the way I write I used to take me I don't know almost two weeks to write a sales letter when I learned this process I was able to write entire sales letter in about two hours It would normally take me two weeks and they convert ten times better Okay, so this is an example of the dot-com secrets X intro because see how I written it see knows how short the senses are They're all like incomplete sentences that pull you from thing to thing to thing and it's just it's very fast to read and it sucks You in so I'm gonna read it to you. It says I've got a quick question for you Have you ever wanted to work from home or own your own business? My guess is this you saw someone online promising you riches untold but there's a catch Doesn't seem like there's always a catch yet to pay hundreds or thousands of dollars to get started right before you make any money Don't they know that's why you're here because you need money Doesn't seem like that if all those gurus courses and software work so good and they really believe in them Why don't they just give you their money making system and then after you've made money then you'd get paid Okay, you see that So copy speed very fast speed and you see how I transition all the way to boom. Okay, I was talking to Perry Belcher who's one of the best online salespeople of all time And he was telling me that every time they have a video sales letter or something that is trying to get somebody to believe two Concepts or three concepts it fails since they're trying to then believe one thing just one core message It always is a winner. So the first section we talked about if you remember we talked about About designing your dream business second one is getting your dream clients And then how to communicate to them through your through your attractive character And now we're gonna be focusing on funneleology, which is the study of funnels like these sales and marketing funnels You probably heard people drop those terms before but what are they? How do they work? And so I literally spent the last year So just going through all the sales funds we've ever done and really dissecting them and breaking them apart and seeing What worked and what didn't work and why they worked and and how they all work and from there I really learned a lot of cool stuff and I wanted to share with you because I think there's things that are gonna be a ton of Value for you. So that's what the funneleology section is the first thing we'll talk about are the seven phases of a funnel Okay, and I'm guessing that four or five these phases you probably never even thought about but it's all at all types back We talked about earlier with the value add like all these pieces tie in together. Okay, so the seven phases of a funnel This is where it kind of starts. All right, the left hand side is It's phase number one is your traffic temperature Okay, I'm gonna talk about this a little bit more here in a minute, but traffic temperature what I mean by that is is is Actually, I'm gonna I'm gonna show this other image because it'll help me illustrate it better We'll come back and cover this in a lot more detail in a minute, but this is this will help so when you have any any Person who's gonna come in potentially by your product they go through this They go through this little spectrum of awareness. Okay in the bottom. There's unaware right they have no idea they've got an issue Okay, then you move up to a problem where like they know they've got a problem And then solution where means means they they're they're a little more advanced and they know there's a problem They've also they're aware of different solutions. They're trying to pick which solution, right? Product where means they're aware. There's a what they're aware. There's a problem there They know a bunch solutions and they know which which product they want And then most aware is like someone who could be teaching this stuff This is probably where most of you guys are in your market, right? And so there's this the spectrum and people are coming at all sorts of different angles now It's interesting in my business most of the people that I that that I know especially in the internet marketing world They're selling people to people that are product aware So they're really good at converting you probably heard this before converting warm or hot traffic, right? It's very easy for them to convert, but they can't convert cold traffic They can't figure out how to do it and the reason why is because there's the way I speak to somebody who's warm Who's solution aware who just needs who's just trying to pick a different solutions way different than the way I speak somebody who's Unaware or problem aware, okay? So problem where is for example Let's say in the make money market okay unaware means I have no idea that there's even a possibility to make money I just I'm here. I'm happy and life's life's hunky-dory that person's really hard to sell Okay, but then something at this point where like I got no money So they've got a problem and they're very aware of that problem. Okay, they're problem aware So if I come to this person who's problem aware and I'm like hey, I'm gonna show you how to set up an email auto responder What's that problem where a person can say? You say I'm trying to make money. What does that have to do with anything? What's an email auto responder, right? They're aware of the problem. They have they have no idea about any of the other stuff Okay, now move it up this this thing now this somebody who who is aware of the solution They know they need to make money and they know they got a bill to list They just got to pick which auto responder they want to have so now there's solution where they're warm traffic So I can communicate this person different I can use different language different verbs different things because they're solution aware Okay, and then they begin to move up to product aware, which means they know they've got the product They're trying to figure out how to use it and then yeah So when I talk about cold traffic warm traffic or hot traffic traffic temperatures is what kind of people are my am I targeting with the Traffic I'm driving. Okay, my targeting people that that are just problem where like something they know They know they need or they know they're trying to make money or they know they're trying to lose weight Or they know that whatever that issue is if that's the case I'm gonna communicate that person differently than someone who's solution where or product aware Okay, so cold traffic warm traffic hot traffic Typically cold traffic is like I'm buying ads on sites. They don't know me warm traffic is typically like I Have a partner or friend who already has a list and they're promoting me and hot is like your own traffic your audience Okay, all right. Let me come back to this Okay, so phase one is traffic temperature understanding this ad that I'm placing out here Where's that as it isn't to my own list? It's my own list I'm gonna communicate differently versus my joint venture partners warm list or cold Facebook ads or cold Google PVC ads, right? Okay, so that's the first step is understanding my traffic now my second phase is what I call the pre-frame bridge Okay, this is the frame that somebody enters your website in Okay, I learned this concept initially from a guy named Mark Joider and he won my very first mentors and Still one of the most the smartest marketing guys I've ever met But he he when I first was studying with him he told me something that had a big impact on me He said Russell not all clicks are created equal. I said, okay, what does that mean? He said well think about it If you go to a website and you see this ad that says hey Russell Brunson is a scam artist He stole my money. He took everything I had and left me with nothing He's a horrible person. He's about to launch this new product click here to check it out And you came to my website through that pre-frame bridge. What's gonna happen to you come right here? Nothing good. You're not gonna convert me well at all, right? But if you come through a print frame bridge that says hey Russell Brunson His coaching program is the best thing in the world. It changed my life I was able to lose weight and get more girls and make more money and everything became better because of him Everything is awesome. Russell's the best click here to check out his new program. They come through that frame How does that affect what's gonna happen here? It's like night and day so understanding this pre-frame. This is the the process they go through before they hit your website You understand that you can you can manipulate that and make Make the conversion that everything else in this process become better. Okay, so a hot list They already they already love me. They know me to like me. They're on my list So my pre-frame is typically very very short usually I'm sending an email out or a blog post saying hey I got this new thing check it out. Okay, because it's warm temperature This little bridge can be very very short to go over here warm traffic is usually a joint venture partner So let's say for example one of my joint venture partners is like Tony Robbins So Tony would go and write an email to his list and say hey, and so this is the pre-frame bridge Tony said hey Russell's awesome This is great blah blah blah blah check this thing out The bridge is a little bit longer than my own list but not much and then boom they come over here And now that he set that pre-frame now they're gonna buy my stuff Hey cold traffic now I'm buying out on Facebook. They have no idea why I am they're they're looking at something else on my face Pops up. I'm trying to get to click on me, right now from here if I drive straight to my squeeze page Typically, it's not gonna convert. So I gotta warm that person up I gotta send them to maybe an article or a blog post or something where I'm gonna provide some value to him so that They warms them up makes them want to enter this relationship with me So I put him through that kind of a pre-frame and then I push them to my site and now I can convert that kind of a person Okay, people always gonna be Russell. I can't convert cold traffic Okay, it's because you're not warming them up You got to take them through this pre-frame bridge to get them to the point where they know you they like you they trust you enough Okay, they're really good at driving warm traffic and hot traffic And then it's so easy because you got these these existing audiences that you're just converting but cold traffic is a different game Okay, you're driving a different different pre-frame and then the way you communicate the audience is different For example in the weight or the how to make money market cold traffic My headline over here has got to be something about how to make money whereas hot traffic You'd be like hey how to make money on webinars, right? Because they already know they want to make money they just looking for the the solution Okay, the solution to where their product where things like that. Okay phase number one traffic temperature phase number two is your pre-frame bridge Phase number three now all this traffic. I'm sending here I gotta find out who of all my traffic is actually willing to give me their email address. I'm qualifying my subscribers Okay, and we do that through a squeeze page. We talked about a lot so far today So you through a squeeze page to put their email in a submit boom now I've gotten their email address now I know who my subscribers are and because of that I'm gonna spend time and energy to market to them I'll send them emails. I'll do a bunch of other stuff, but they're not willing to put their email address in not gonna waste my time with them Okay, now as soon as I've qualified my subscribers immediately afterwards. I want to find out who my buyers are Okay, one of my mentors Dan Kennedy told me said a buyer is a buyer is a buyer Someone who buys once will continue to buy over and over and over and over and over again So when you understand that you understand that you just added a thousand people to your list Don't you want to know who those buyers are would you treat them a little differently? Did you do different things for them? I know I would So I want to find out who those buyers are as quick as possible I want to find out who is willing to take their wallet out of the pocket open it up Pull a credit card out and after they got a credit card hand type in the digits and click submit Because the person that's willing to do that once is gonna do it over and over and over again So I'm gonna identify who those people are immediately so as soon as they opt in I'm gonna sell them something and usually for me It's gonna be really really a sexy hot offer Really cheap really low price point because I don't want any barriers Everyone who's my buyers so I can get them in this buying process or keep buying over and over and over again And that's phase number four is I'm qualifying my buyers Okay, now that I've got my buyers qualified know who my buyers are now when I identify who my hyperactive buyers are Okay, my hyperactive are people like me who buy a lot of stuff over and over and over again Okay, so from there that's when you start selling them upsells. Okay, you just some buys a product I'm sure you guys have bought stuff online before after my buys. There's like wait Special offer for you. Do you want this thing for an extra 97 bucks? Hey, do you want this thing for extra 500 bucks? It's what we're doing is we're offering more expensive offers to find out how good of a buyer You are how hyperactive are you because the person who spends $100 in upsell I'm gonna spend more time in F1 on this. I'm gonna only pay $5 for a for a front-end product, right? So I'm doing is I'm segmenting my my buyers finding out who are my good buyers my better buyers my best buyers Can I do that through upsells and I'm identifying my hyperactive? All right, everything right here phases one to five all this stuff happens on your on your website and the initial sales flow Okay, I talked about earlier. I called it point of sale. So the point of sale this all happens Usually within like five minutes of itself. It's a very fast process Now after step five is done and we've got the customer we got the buyer now step six and seven this this process goes on for Days months weeks years Okay, number six. I'm trying to age and send the relationship. Can we talk about value ladder? I'm trying to start slowly moving them up the value ladder give more value provide more value offer more things I'm gonna be sending them up this value ladder and somewhere along the line though on the internet We can only do so much stuff. Okay online. I really struggle selling things more than about two or three thousand dollars online She gets really hard the resistance is really high. So somewhere along that line I can't age them any I can't excuse me. I can't send them any higher than that It's what I do is I have to change the selling environment Okay, so for me what I do is we either get them on the phone We send them something in the mail or we do a seminar or a webinar something I gotta change that environment because it takes more for me to sell a 5,000 or 10,000 or 25,000 or a million dollar package. You've got to change the environment So somewhere along the line you got to you got to take them off this online thing and change the environment And now you can sell them the earth your hire taking more expensive things Okay, and so that's kind of how the funnel works. Those are the seven phases of the funnel. All right now. What's cool I'll show you kind of the next step is that when I'm building out a funnel This is the first thing I look at okay, what are the phases we're taking them through now that I've got that now I come back and they say okay for this phase number number three right here. What are my options? Okay? We got squeeze pager, but there's probably four or five different ways. I can qualify my subscribers, right? And then buyers there's four or five ways I can qualify my buyers a bunch of ways I can send my audience all these things are different ways I can do that So because of that we created what I call the 23 building blocks of a funnel Okay, and this is literally I spent a long time going through all of our funnels I realized that I was doing this over and over and over again, but I was doing it in different ways Okay, so right here the pre frame bridge. So the pre frame bridge we talked about there's there's one two three four five six seven There's seven types of pre frames that we use in our company Okay, pre frame number one is a quiz You guys ever noticed on my list you've probably been you've had me send you to a quiz before right? I send a lot of traffic to a quiz. Okay, it's a pre frame and the quiz by the way I think right now is probably the best pre frame that we're using I don't know if they'll be the same way in a year from now right now quizzes are working like crazy. I don't know why Articles if I send some an article that's a pre frame If if I go to like a news website or a news blogs or something like that Again a blog video sometimes some people do YouTube video from there I send more to my to my squeeze pages through email or through a pre-sell page Okay, so I'm creating my new campaign my new funnel I say came from my pre frame bridge which one I want or on a quiz an article I just pick one of these okay, you may say Russell. What's the best? It just depends. I just try different things Sometimes this is gonna be the best sometimes this is gonna be the best you just kind of just play with it It's it's a this whole marketing thing. There's a science and there's an art right everyone's like Russell I just want the science. What's the best thing? But you have to understand there's the art part too And if if it was just a complete science then every funnel would work perfectly if you just copied it But it doesn't because people they they get used to it They start seeing the same thing over and over and over again that stops working And so it's the art behind it that makes it change and be different So if I have a list and I'm sending quizzes every bunch times eventually quizzes will stop working So they gotta send out a video the next time or send out a blog I'm just changing this pre frame and seeing what my audience responds to okay from Facebook one from Facebook to a quiz Works awesome. Okay going from YouTube to another video works awesome going from one blog to another blog works awesome a lot Times will match where we're driving the traffic to with the type of pre frame bridge. We're using as well Okay, so those are the building blocks for a pre frame Okay, next step in our process trying to qualify our subscribers. So how do I do that? Okay, squeeze pages when we showed you a lot. It's an easy way to qualify subscribers pop-ups we use a lot Okay, a squeeze pop is a is like a button on my side I click a button and it pops up and then I put them in their name and email address in there Okay, a free plus shipping some of you guys have seen like our free Dotcom secrets labs book. We have a free plus shipping where step number one You give me your shipping address and your email and step number two we bill you when they fill out step number one They're added to my list. So I use a two-step Free plus shipping process to qualify my subscribers Sometimes I drive people to a webinar registration. They read sure for my webinar and boom now their subscriber Sometimes I give people a free account something. Hey, they'll go create a free account over here They create a free account and boom it create it turns them into a subscriber Sometimes I have an exit pop where they're on my site and it exit pops out. They fill it in and boom they join my list Okay, so those are a bunch of different building blocks for qualifying subscribers Okay Now the reason my colleagues getting building blocks like when I was designing this I was thinking about Legos Like we went to Lego land my kids in a while ago and you know, they go and they build Legos And so they stack them on and build this whole thing and I was thinking about this like this is kind of like Legos I'm building the funnel. That's a hundred ninety seven dollars Now we spent a ton of time and energy creating this website and writing copy doing an amazing job and from that We only got one percent conversion rate, which actually is pretty good when all said done So for a hundred visitors we're making they went to this website. We were making one sale, which is a hundred ninety seven dollars Can you that that you may think that's not that bad? It's not but check out what happened when we just kind of tweak this offer We took one piece of this hundred ninety seven dollar product the best part the one thing that people want the most and we pulled it out What we did is we put it out in front so we had a hundred visitors and we gave that thing away for free We gave away on a free CD sometimes on a free book sometimes on a free mp3 player just something we away for free We just charge shipping handling and from the same hundred visitors instead of getting 1% conversion We get 8% that would say yes to that and get the free CD Okay, and what's interesting is the psychology behind this through somebody said that very first yes To give them say yes the second thing is a million times easier because of those eight people who took the first thing on average We get about 25% of those people to buy the next offer in our value ladder right the next value in our in our funnel Which is the exact same hundred ninety seven dollar product the sales letter is much simpler on this and I'll show you guys the script We use here in a minute. I'll give him credit for it But this is what the who what why house script looks like Okay, you're basically gonna answer four questions for someone when they come to your website So it comes to website and for for example for my dot-com secrets labs book They come to the page and and I can actually four things the first one is who so I say hey, my name is Russell Brunson Okay, I just told them who? Then tell them why they need it the reason why you need this book is if you got a website and you're struggling to get it to Convert and to turn your traffic into visitors just because you're Converting it's just because you set it up wrong you structured it the wrong way This books gonna show you every single split test we ran the last two years What worked what didn't work and you can just look at it and copy it for your own business and see huge success That's why you need it Then how can you get it? How you can use just fill out the form on the side Let me know where to ship this to and and then click submit and then we will send you this book in the mail right away Okay, please write who what why how who are you here? There's the intro There's the content and then there's the the pitch the close so the intro again We start with the bold promise give them a hook to the end something about If you stand the end this webinar going to give you this this cool prize or this bonus or whatever We command attention we tell them to put away skype or Facebook You try to get their attention we qualify ourselves and why you're an expert why they should listen to us We future-pays them about what's gonna happen if they if they consume this webinar and gain from it future-pays them Right, then we jump into the content of the webinar most people When they do their webinars, they're trying to again. They're trying to teach people five or six or seven things For a webinar to work really really well same thing that we talked about all the other scripts you guys You're focused on one thing. What's the one core message that you need to? To get your audience to believe to buy what it is you're selling Okay, and then everything else wraps around that's for us. We pick one core thing Okay, and usually this one core thing is We tied to headline this is how to blank without blank If you can fit that if you can fit your thing you're selling into that little framework It works, okay For example one of our recent webinars was high-ticket secrets. It was how to sell high-ticket products without picking up the phone How to do what without what? Okay, that's the one thing and then I go and I share secrets that There aren't separate secrets or secrets that strengthen the one thing the one core argument Okay, and and each of these secrets is to break and rebuild their pattern So if my if my one thing again is about how to sell high-ticket things without selling on a phone Secret number one is probably gonna be something it's something it's a pattern there It's a belief they have in their mind Okay, they believe right now that to sell high-ticket things they have to be on the phone secret number one Here's how I do it without a phone. Okay, I'm gonna share them the secret I'm gonna in that process break their belief that they have to have the phone And I'm gonna rebuild it with the truth with the truth that I want them to understand and believe The secret number two is maybe I can know I can do that, but it's gonna take a lot of traffic So secret number two is how to do this with almost no traffic I got to break their belief and then rebuild their belief patterns to what I need them to believe to buy my thing and Secret number three is the same thing Okay, so finding out what their belief patterns are that they're keeping them from buying my thing I show them the pattern I break it and I rebuild it what I need them to believe Okay, that's the content section of your webinar and from there we transition I say let me ask you a question I just showed you some cool stuff But how would you like for me to help you do this and we transition to our clothes and again? I could spend days on this clothes. I'm not gonna go through it in a hole, but just to kind of give you Just kind of give you a really quick rundown The first thing we do the the overall clothes Process is what I call a stack I learned this from a guy named Armand Moran The stack has literally made me at least ten million dollars in the last five or six years. It's how I do every one of my presentations What it does is basically what I what I'm sharing my offer I share here's component number one and I talk about it and I here's component number two But you notice I stack them I share component number three and I stack them again number four stack them So if you ever look at my my presentations, you'll notice that that in fact It's almost humorous sometimes like if first thing you get is this that means blah blah blah blah So that means the first thing you get is this and then here's second number thing and I keep retelling this by time I got the end you've heard me stack all the the the components of the offer like ten times But what we found is if you don't do it that way People only remember the last bonus or last thing you told them And so they're justifying the price of what you're selling based on the last thing you told them And so you want before you tell them any kind of price You want them to see the entire offer everything they're getting in a huge stack. That's what we call it the stack Okay, and then we pitch it high we do our if all statements just like I showed you guys in the last script You price high drop price low and we ever called action Okay, then in throughout this throughout the stack There's a bunch of different closes and I'm not gonna go through these right now But these are a bunch of different clothes that we use all each I'll drop one or two or three. Well, I'll probably drop I don't know maybe seven or eight of these different closes throughout this whole process and If you Google most these you can find them some of these are mine some of them I learned from from other stage presenters some I learned from a lot of different people But if you Google some of these you'll find out what these closes are We also have a have a product at a perfect webinar calm that we I go into a lot more detail about this so That's kind of how we do a webinar pitch All right, so that's that's the That's the regular webinar now. So usually there's a four video series we're trying to provide as much value as possible for somebody Video number one is called the wow and how The number two is the transformational education video number three is the ownership experience video number four is the offer Okay, so that's what the product launch funnel looks like Now this is what the scripts look like inside of it. Okay, so this is the high-ticket script Looks like this and I know there's a lot of steps here I'm not our sales person. We did a whole literally we did a Probably 20 hours of training in our high-ticket secrets course that goes through this But this is kind of it. It's a two-set script First is a is the first person that that calls is what we call our setter and their job is to find out if If the person's gonna be good fit for our coaching program So they they they basically call a person up they introduce themselves They ask so much of questions about them about their business They go through the blast which is kind of this process kind of show a bunch of stuff What's happening?
